title: Halo Hunters and Zeruel
wikitext:
[]: Halo Hunters and Zeruel             Quote Post by V » Mon Feb 25, 2008 6:54 pm http://nikon.bungie.org/screenshots/cdmag700/shot3big.jpg I've been thinking about this a lot, and the extendable orange necks of the Hunters in the Halo series REALLY seems like its based on Zeruel's retractable cartiliginous neck from Episode 19. Others have already noticed that in their "contracted" form, they kind of look like Jet Alone. And we know the production team guys are Eva fans....

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Feb 25, 2008 7:25 pm This is JUST like when Ursus said that the Covenant Grunts looked like Jet Alone.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Feb 25, 2008 7:54 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: This is JUST like when Ursus said that the Covenant Grunts looked like Jet Alone.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 25, 2008 8:10 pm Who's to say there isn't an Eva fan in Bungie? It's possible. We don't know how many Bungie crew are fans of Anime other than Ghost in the Shell, do we?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 25, 2008 8:16 pm I believe they are based on these: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marathon_Trilogy#S.27pht A previous Bungie game from 1994, which predates Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 25, 2008 8:25 pm Of course, there were the old Pfhor hunters as well, but I don't know how their heads were shaped. Since so much of Halo references Marathon, it's possible that any similarities with Eva are just a coincidence. It's hard to say with these things. You might say that the Covenant Elite head design was based off Eva-01 (With a split chin added), but there could be quite a few aliens with helmet and head designs like that, making any comparisions pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Feb 25, 2008 9:22 pm BobBQ wrote: Sachiel_13 wrote: This is JUST like when Ursus said that the Covenant Grunts looked like Jet Alone.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Feb 25, 2008 9:42 pm Ornette wrote: I believe they are based on these: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marathon_Trilogy#S.27pht A previous Bungie game from 1994, which predates Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Tue Feb 26, 2008 10:58 am There are similaritys but i dont think there are any links, their just generic freaky looking alien things.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 26, 2008 1:37 pm Ahh, Halos. Horrible creatures, I avoid them when I can What exactly is Zeruelish on that critter?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Feb 26, 2008 8:44 pm the neck

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 26, 2008 8:58 pm Even E.T. had an extendable neck. It really doesn't prove anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 26, 2008 9:15 pm "Sinewy with a flat head on top" is sort of Zeruel-ish. I don't think it needs to be a reference or anything, though.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 26, 2008 9:33 pm More accurate would be "sinewy with a face on top" because Zeruel has essentially a face at the end of a neck, rather than a proper head. The only two Angels (Children of Adam) that have real heads are Shamshel and Sandalphon, and both of them lack necks. Never mind, I digress.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 27, 2008 7:07 am ARGH SEMANTICS YOU KNOW WHAT I MEAN. Also, you forgot Gaghiel and Arael.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Feb 27, 2008 9:43 am Yeah, Gaghiel's would classify as a head, even though it merges more into his body pod than Sandy's does. I thought Arael was just a pair of wings joined in the middle with a floating core to boot.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Wed Feb 27, 2008 12:58 pm If it were true it would be quite interesting to see if anything else in the halo games had a likeness to stuff in eva but I think its just a bit of over fanwankery on V's part.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 28, 2008 12:34 am UrsusArctos wrote: Yeah, Gaghiel's would classify as a head, even though it merges more into his body pod than Sandy's does.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Feb 28, 2008 2:19 am Reichu wrote: That doesn't make it any less a head. Plenty of animals have streamlined head-body units -- check out marine vertebrates, for starts.
summary: []: Halo Hunters and Zeruel             Quote Post by V » Mon Feb 25, 2008 6:54 pm http://nikon.bungie.org/screenshots/cdmag700/shot3big.jpg I've been thinking about this a lot, and the extendable orange necks of the Hunters in the Halo series REALLY seems like its based on Zeruel's retractable cartiliginous neck from Episode 19. Others have already noticed that in their "contracted" form, they kind of look like Jet Alone.
